data.body:
## Description
When /dso:sprint invokes /dso:implementation-plan via the Skill tool, the skill completes successfully (outputs STATUS:complete with task IDs) but control is not automatically returned to the sprint orchestrator. The user must manually prompt to continue.

## Reproduction
1. Run /dso:sprint on an epic with stories needing implementation planning
2. Sprint invokes /dso:implementation-plan via Skill tool for a story
3. implementation-plan completes and outputs STATUS:complete TASKS:<ids> STORY:<id>
4. Expected: sprint orchestrator continues to next story or next phase
5. Actual: execution stops, user must prompt to continue

## Impact
Sprint execution requires manual intervention between each implementation-plan invocation, breaking the automated orchestration flow. For epics with multiple stories (e.g., 3 Layer 0 stories), this requires 3 manual prompts just to complete the implementation planning gate.

## Notes
This may be inherent to how the Skill tool works — each skill invocation loads as a new user message, and the orchestrator must re-orient after receiving the skill output. Similar to the known CONTROL FLOW WARNING in sprint Step 10 (commit workflow).
